$comment: Spaarke AI Platform Unification R6 (Phase B Wave B-G2 task 033, D-B-04) — Workspace-destination Summarize playbook. CREATE-not-migrate path chosen 2026-06-09 after prior 033 sub-agent surfaced that summarize-document-for-workspace@v1 did NOT exist on Spaarke Dev (full discovery in projects/spaarke-ai-platform-unification-r6/notes/task-033-migration-evidence.md). Per Q5 RE-SHAPED principle (project CLAUDE.md): outputSchema is INTRINSIC to the action; destination + widgetType are PER-PLAYBOOK NODE routing config. This playbook REUSES the existing SUM-CHAT@v1 action (GUID eeb05bfd-1260-f111-ab0b-70a8a59455f4) — same model, same JPS prompt, same output shape (tldr/summary/keywords/entities) — and adds workspace-destination routing on the node. Same shape, two destinations: chat sibling (summarize-document-for-chat@v1) routes to inline chat; this playbook routes to a workspace tab via the schema-aware StructuredOutputStreamWidget (R6 tasks 040/041). The FR-24 render-routing dedup ensures one user intent fires ONE playbook, eliminating the R5 SC-18 duplicate-fire path that surfaced the rendering bugs.
$comment-publication: Published as: (1) ZERO new sprk_analysisaction rows — references existing SUM-CHAT@v1 by actionCode FK; (2) one sprk_analysisplaybook row (name=summarize-document-for-workspace@v1, isSystemPlaybook=true, sprk_playbooktype=0); (3) one sprk_playbooknode row (single AiAnalysis node, FK to existing SUM-CHAT@v1, sprk_configjson carries destination=workspace + widgetType=structured-output-stream per task 031 NodeRoutingConfig contract).
$comment-design: Option A (shared action, new playbook) chosen over Option B (new action with workspace-tuned prompt). Rationale: the SUM-CHAT@v1 outputSchema (tldr: string[]; summary: string; keywords: string; entities: { organizations: string[]; persons: string[] }) is EXACTLY what StructuredOutputStreamWidget.SUMMARIZE_SCHEMA consumes (verified in src/client/shared/Spaarke.AI.Widgets/src/widgets/workspace/StructuredOutputStreamWidget.tsx lines 264-300). The model produces the same content regardless of render surface; the surface choice is a routing concern, not a content concern. This aligns with project CLAUDE.md Pillar 5 'Q5 RE-SHAPED' binding rule: 'outputSchema on the ACTION (intrinsic shape); destination + widgetType on the NODE config (per-playbook routing); a single action can be referenced by multiple playbooks.' Authoring debt is also lower: 0 new actions vs 1.
$comment-widgetType-wire: widgetType value 'structured-output-stream' is the kebab-case registry key from src/client/shared/Spaarke.AI.Widgets/src/widgets/workspace/register-structured-output-stream-widget.ts line 30 (STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_STREAM_WIDGET_TYPE = 'structured-output-stream' as const). This is the wire-format string the WorkspaceWidgetRegistry resolves at render time. The C# NodeRoutingConfig.WidgetType field is intentionally a free-form string (Models/Ai/NodeRoutingConfig.cs:148) to allow extension without recompile. The JSON Schema (Models/Ai/node-routing-config.schema.json) example uses 'Summary' as an illustration only; the actual valid value is the registry key the host registers under, which is 'structured-output-stream' for R5/R6 streaming structured-output rendering.
$comment-actions-empty: Intentionally empty. This playbook does NOT seed a new action — it REFERENCES the existing SUM-CHAT@v1 action (seeded by summarize-document-for-chat.playbook.json in R5). The Deploy-Playbook.ps1 harness collects all node-level actionCodes into allActionCodes at line 361 and resolves them against sprk_analysisactions via the SUM-CHAT@v1 alternate key (line 396-405). If SUM-CHAT@v1 is missing on the target environment, the pre-flight check fails loudly at line 446-510 BEFORE creating the playbook row — no orphaned rows on failure.

playbook.name: summarize-document-for-workspace@v1
playbook.description: Workspace-destination Summarize playbook (R6 task 033 / D-B-04). Single AiAnalysis node executes the EXISTING SUM-CHAT@v1 action against 1-N session-scoped files; emits the SAME structured DocumentAnalysisResult-shaped output (tldr, summary, keywords, entities) as the chat sibling — but routes via destination=workspace + widgetType=structured-output-stream to a workspace tab rendered by the schema-aware StructuredOutputStreamWidget (R6 tasks 040 + 041). Pairs with summarize-document-for-chat@v1 (chat sibling, same action, destination=chat). One model output, two render surfaces; routing dedup at the FR-24 render-routing level.
playbook.$comment-type: sprk_playbooktype=0 (AiAnalysis) — matches summarize-document-for-chat@v1 + universal-ingest@v1 + predict-matter-cost@v1 convention. System-owned (isSystemPlaybook=true), not user-authored.

sprk_configjson.$comment-cacheTtl: Cache TTL = 0 — same rationale as chat sibling: per-session and per-file-set; the file set changes mid-session as users upload/remove files. Idempotency comes from session-files index isolation + per-invocation file-set targeting.

sessionId.description: Chat session GUID. Scopes retrieval to the spaarke-session-files index slice for this session. Required for tenant + session isolation per ADR-014.

tenantId.description: Tenant ID for multi-tenant isolation per ADR-014. Required on every spaarke-session-files index query.

fileIds.description: OPTIONAL — subset of session-uploaded files to summarize. When absent, all files in ChatSession.UploadedFiles are summarized as a combined-summary.

style.description: OPTIONAL — natural-language style hint passed through to the action's system prompt (e.g., 'executive', 'detailed'). The action's JPS prompt interprets this; absent style defaults to the prompt's default rendering.

workspaceTabId.description: OPTIONAL — pre-existing workspace tab id to stream into. When absent, the workspace shell opens a new tab via allowMultiple=true per FR-06 (each Summarize invocation gets its own tab so users can compare side-by-side).

playbook.$comment-scopes: Scopes left empty intentionally — matches summarize-document-for-chat@v1 + universal-ingest@v1 + predict-matter-cost@v1 convention. The single node's executor (AiAnalysisNodeExecutor for actionType=0) is auto-discovered by NodeExecutorRegistry; per-node scope binding via actionCode FK is sufficient. The node-level actionCode='SUM-CHAT@v1' below is collected into allActionCodes by Deploy-Playbook.ps1 line 361 and resolves against the EXISTING SUM-CHAT@v1 row (no new action seeded by this file).

nodes.name: summarize
nodes.$comment-actionCode: Resolved by Deploy-Playbook.ps1 line 396-405 against sprk_analysisactions filtered by sprk_actioncode='SUM-CHAT@v1'. This file's actions[] array is EMPTY — the action is expected to ALREADY exist (seeded by summarize-document-for-chat.playbook.json in R5). The deploy harness sets sprk_playbooknode.sprk_actionid to the resolved GUID (line 737-740). Pre-flight check at line 446-510 will fail loudly if SUM-CHAT@v1 is missing.
nodes.$comment-actionType: ExecutorType.AiAnalysis = 0; handled by AiAnalysisNodeExecutor (existing platform executor — no new node executor needed per R6 NFR-08). The SUM-CHAT@v1 action carries sprk_systemprompt with the JPS-formatted Summarize prompt + the structured-outputs JSON Schema (declaration order: tldr → summary → keywords → entities).

configJson.$comment-routing: destination + widgetType implement R6 Pillar 5 / FR-27 (Q5 RE-SHAPED). 'destination=workspace' (kebab-case per Models/Ai/NodeRoutingConfig.cs NodeDestinationJsonConverter) routes this node's output to the workspace surface. 'widgetType=structured-output-stream' (kebab-case wire format = STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_STREAM_WIDGET_TYPE constant from src/client/shared/Spaarke.AI.Widgets/src/widgets/workspace/register-structured-output-stream-widget.ts:30) tells the workspace shell which widget to mount the stream into. The schema-aware widget (R6 tasks 040 + 041) will read the SUM-CHAT@v1 action's outputSchema and render array-typed tldr as bullets + object-typed entities as labeled key-value blocks (fixes R5 SC-18 Gap C raw-JSON rendering).
configJson.$comment-executor: AiAnalysisNodeExecutor receives session + tenant + file scope via parameters. The actual document text retrieval is performed by the orchestrator (R6 task 025 PlaybookExecutionEngine path via SessionSummarizeOrchestrator) using the spaarke-session-files index slice before invoking this playbook. Structured Outputs mode + IncrementalJsonParser emit progressive FieldDelta SSE events as the model streams (tldr first, then summary, keywords, entities — declaration order per R5 task 006 spike); StructuredOutputStreamWidget renders the deltas in the workspace tab.

dependsOnGraph.$comment: Single-node playbook — no edges. Mirrors the structural shape of multi-node playbooks for tooling consistency.

acceptanceCriteria.$comment: Mirrors R6 task 033 POML <acceptance-criteria> (revised for CREATE path 2026-06-09).
criteria: Playbook publishes successfully — sprk_analysisplaybook row + 1 sprk_playbooknode row in Dataverse, name='summarize-document-for-workspace@v1', isSystemPlaybook=true, sprk_playbooktype=0

Node references EXISTING SUM-CHAT@v1 action — sprk_playbooknode._sprk_actionid_value = eeb05bfd-1260-f111-ab0b-70a8a59455f4 (resolved by deploy harness from actionCode='SUM-CHAT@v1' against sprk_actioncode alternate key)
Node sprk_configjson includes destination='workspace' (kebab-case per NodeRoutingConfig contract) and widgetType='structured-output-stream' (kebab-case registry key)

outputSchema reuse — this playbook does NOT seed a new sprk_analysisaction row; it references SUM-CHAT@v1 whose sprk_outputschemajson was populated by R6 task 032 (Migrate-SumChatActionOutputSchema.ps1)
Re-running Deploy-Playbook.ps1 without -Force is idempotent (skip-by-name; no new rows created)
BFF publish-size delta = 0 MB (data-only authoring; no .cs/.csproj/.NuGet change)
